title: Batch Settlement Window Velocity Exploitation with Amount Stepping
narrative: Fraudster exploits merchant batch settlement timing by executing rapid-fire transactions in the 30-45 minute window before typical end-of-day settlement (often 23:00-23:45 local merchant time). Attack begins with $47-$89 test transactions, then escalates to $340-$480 range within 8-12 minutes once authorization success confirmed. Fraudster targets merchants with known daily batch cycles (gas stations MCC 5541, convenience stores MCC 5499) where auth holds clear at settlement rather than real-time. Pattern shows card1_txn_count_6h jumping from 0 to 9-14 transactions, all within txn_hour 22-23. Transactions cluster at 3-4 different merchants within same geographic addr1, suggesting route planning. The card1_amt_zscore spikes from baseline 0.2 to 4.8+ as amounts escalate. Fraudster banks on delayed merchant reconciliation post-settlement allowing maximum extraction before card blocking.
indicators: 9-14 transactions within 35-minute window, all occurring txn_hour 22-23
Amount progression pattern: 2-3 txns $47-$89, then 6-9 txns $340-$480
card1_txn_count_6h increases from 0 to 9-14 with no prior history
All transactions at MCC 5541 or 5499 merchants within same addr1 region
card1_amt_zscore escalates from <1.0 to >4.5 within 15-minute span
Transactions cluster within 45 minutes before typical 23:00-00:00 batch settlement window
dist1 values show 8-25 mile progression suggesting planned geographic route
summary: Multiple transactions rapidly escalate from small test amounts ($47-$89) to larger charges ($340-$480) at gas stations or convenience stores, all clustered in the late evening hours (10pm-midnight) across nearby locations within a single region.
reasoning: The attacker is racing against the merchant's end-of-day settlement window, knowing that authorization holds won't immediately deduct funds and betting they can maximize stolen value before the card gets blocked during overnight reconciliation.
indicator_explanations: Card suddenly fires 9-14 transactions in late-night half-hour burst
Purchases start small to test limits then jump to high-dollar amounts
Card goes from completely dormant to intense activity in six hours
All hits target gas stations or convenience stores in same neighborhood
Spending amounts spike from normal to extreme within quarter-hour
Attack timed to complete just before midnight batch processing cutoff
Transactions march across 8-25 miles like following a planned route
